introLines.text: I was driving on the highway last week and I saw lights in the distance that didn't make sense.
Vern, I'm a delivery driver. I'm on the road all night. Last Tuesday I saw something over the fields.
So I was out on Route 9, middle of nowhere, and there were these lights on the horizon doing impossible things.

detailLines.text: Three lights in a row, just hovering. Then they shot off in different directions simultaneously. Like, instant.
I pulled over to watch. They were maybe a mile out? Hard to tell at night. Moving in formation, then splitting up.
It was too far to get a good video. Just looks like dots on my phone. But I know what I saw.

defenseLines.text: No airport out there, Vern. I've driven that route a hundred times. Never seen planes do that.
Drones? Maybe. But they were big. And fast. Military drones? I don't know.
I'm not saying it was aliens. But it wasn't normal aircraft. I'd stake my job on it.

acceptanceLines.text: Yeah, that's what I figured too. Just needed someone else to say it.
Okay, good. I'm not crazy then. That's a relief.
I might keep a better camera in the truck from now on. Just in case.

conclusionLines.text: Thanks Vern. I'll keep my eyes on the sky during my routes.
If I see it again, I'll call back. Got your number on speed dial now.
Appreciate you taking the call. Stay safe out there.
